title: vachellia tortilis subsp. spirocarpa
intro: Vachellia tortilis subsp. spirocarpa, also known as the 'Mopane' tree or 'White Thorn', is a subspecies of the Vachellia tortilis tree, native to southern Africa. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of this plant include treating fever, pain, and skin conditions, as well as having antifungal and antiseptic properties. The herbal preparations of Vachellia tortilis subsp. spirocarpa include infusions, decoctions, and powders, which can be used to make teas, ointments, and creams for various health applications.
